meta.title: Connecting Next.js to Strapi API
meta.excerpt: Fetch and display Strapi content in your Next.js application

toc.title: 3. Displaying Data in a Server Component

props.text: Configure your Next.js app to connect to Strapi.

props.code:
# Create .env.local in your Next.js project root
STRAPI_URL=http://localhost:1337
STRAPI_TOKEN=your-api-token-here

# Generate an API token in Strapi:
# Settings → API Tokens → Create new API Token
# Name: Next.js Frontend
# Token type: Full access (or custom)
# Copy the token to your .env.local

props.title: 💡 Hint
props.body: Never commit API tokens to git. Add .env.local to your .gitignore file.

props.text: Build a reusable client for fetching Strapi data.

props.title: 📖 Explanation
props.body: We use TypeScript interfaces to ensure type safety. The next.revalidate option enables ISR caching.

props.title: Displaying Data in a Server Component

props.text: Fetch and render Strapi data in a Next.js page.

props.title: 📖 Explanation
props.body: Server Components can directly await async functions. No useEffect or useState needed!

props.text: Handle API errors gracefully with error boundaries.
